title: Silent Replacement of Trusted macOS App Executables
byline:
by
    
    
    
    
    Talal Haj Bakry
     and 
    
    
    Tommy Mysk
excerpt: A vulnerability in macOS allows an attacker to silently replace the main executable of any application downloaded from the web without requiring elevated privileges. As a result, trusted applications can be made to execute attacker-controlled code without triggering security warnings when relaunched. Apple assessed the reported behaviour as not requiring a security fix.
content_html:
A vulnerability in macOS allows an attacker to silently replace the main executable of any application downloaded from the web without requiring elevated privileges. As a result, trusted applications can be made to execute attacker-controlled code without triggering security warnings when relaunched. Apple assessed the reported behaviour as not requiring a security fix.

Affected Platforms#

  • macOS Tahoe 26.0.0 – 26.5.2
  • macOS Golden Gate 27 beta 1, 2, 3, and 4
  • Earlier versions of macOS are likely affected as well, but have not been tested.

Summary#

macOS provides several safeguards to prevent applications and scripts from tampering with other installed applications. In this post, we show a bug in macOS that allows an attacker to:

  • Silently replace an application’s main executable under /Contents/MacOS/ without triggering an authorization prompt.
  • Relaunch the modified application normally, without displaying any security warnings.
  • Impersonate the trusted application in system permission prompts to request access to Keychain secrets and files protected by Transparency, Consent, and Control (TCC), including those inside ~/Desktop and ~/Documents.

The attack requires only code execution as the current user, and does not require elevated privileges.

Summary for Non-Technical Readers#

We found a macOS security issue that Apple looked into but decided not to fix. If you run a malicious app or script on your Mac, an attacker could:

  • Secretly replace trusted apps you already have installed from the web with malicious versions.
  • Request permissions to access private data on behalf of those trusted apps, including files in places you expect to be private, such as your Desktop or Documents folders, or your Keychain.

Replacing a trusted app requires neither your Mac’s password nor any special approval, and it can happen entirely in the background. Accessing protected data still requires your approval, but the macOS system prompts show the trusted app’s name and icon, making the requests appear to come from the real app.

Background#

macOS Application Bundles#

On macOS, apps are distributed as application bundles, which are directory structures that appear as a single .app file in Finder. An application bundle contains the app’s executable, resources such as icons and artwork, embedded frameworks and libraries, and metadata. Apple’s documentation describes the bundle format in more detail.

When a user downloads an app from the Internet and launches it for the first time, macOS verifies its code signature and notarization and applies Gatekeeper policies to determine whether the app is trusted to run.

After the app is installed (for example, by dragging it into /Applications) and opened, macOS also protects the contents of its bundle. Other applications and scripts, even those running with administrator privileges, cannot modify files inside it. macOS blocks any such attempt and alerts the user that it prevented the modification.

Archive and Restore#

We accidentally discovered a scenario in which any application or script running with the current user’s privileges can silently replace the main executable of an application bundle and launch the modified application without triggering a code-signature or security warning.

The issue is reproducible when:

  1. The app was downloaded from the web rather than installed through the Mac App Store. App Store apps are owned by root, while apps downloaded from the web are typically owned by the current user.
  2. The app has already been launched at least once, allowing Gatekeeper to complete its initial verification.
  3. The attacker already has code execution as the current user, for example through a malicious app or downloaded script.

We’ll use Signal as an example. To be clear, this is not a Signal vulnerability. Signal is simply a convenient demonstration target because it is widely trusted and distributed outside the Mac App Store. The same behaviour affects other apps downloaded from the web, including Brave Browser, Cursor, Mullvad Browser, Proton Mail, Slack, Visual Studio Code, Xcode, and many others.

After installing Signal and launching it once, macOS prevents subsequent modifications to its application bundle. You can verify this by running:

% touch /Applications/Signal.app/Contents/MacOS/Signal
touch: /Applications/Signal.app/Contents/MacOS/Signal: Operation not permitted

Even with sudo, you still cannot modify the application’s main executable:

% sudo touch /Applications/Signal.app/Contents/MacOS/Signal
touch: /Applications/Signal.app/Contents/MacOS/Signal: Operation not permitted

However, archiving the application bundle with tar, deleting the original, and extracting the archive back into /Applications changes this behaviour:

% cd /Applications/
% tar cf .Signal.tar Signal.app
% rm -rf Signal.app
% tar xf .Signal.tar -C /Applications

The restored app launches normally, despite being a different copy of the original bundle. What’s surprising is that its contents can be modified without triggering an authorization prompt:

% touch /Applications/Signal.app/Contents/MacOS/Signal
%

It works!

At this point, replacing the app’s main executable is straightforward. The modified app continues to launch, and macOS displays neither a Gatekeeper warning nor any indication that its bundle has been altered. Here’s a minimal example that uses Swift to compile a dummy executable and then replaces Signal’s main executable with it:

# Write a minimal Swift program that shows a window
cat << EOF > /tmp/dummy.swift
import Cocoa

let app = NSApplication.shared
let window = NSWindow(
    contentRect: NSRect(x: 0, y: 0, width: 400, height: 200),
    styleMask: [.titled, .closable, .miniaturizable, .resizable],
    backing: .buffered,
    defer: false
)
window.center()
window.title = "Hi! I'm Signal!"
window.makeKeyAndOrderFront(nil)
app.activate(ignoringOtherApps: true)
app.run()
EOF

# Compile it as a command-line macOS executable
swiftc -framework Cocoa /tmp/dummy.swift -o /tmp/dummy

# Replace the main Signal executable with the dummy binary
cp /tmp/dummy /Applications/Signal.app/Contents/MacOS/Signal

# Launch the now-modified app (should display a window, not real Signal)
open /Applications/Signal.app

This behaviour is consistent with how macOS code signing protects application bundles. The bundle’s resources are sealed by hashes stored in the _CodeSignature/CodeResources file, while the main executable carries its own embedded code signature. Because the archive-and-restore process leaves the bundle resources unchanged, their hashes continue to validate successfully.

The replaced executable, however, is ad hoc signed, and macOS permits ad hoc signed executables to run. As a result, the modified application bundle launches successfully even though its original executable has been replaced.

What appears inconsistent is that macOS continues to recognize the modified bundle as the same trusted app — though not entirely, since the replacement executable still triggers Keychain and TCC prompts, as discussed in the next section. Once the original developer-signed executable has been replaced with an ad hoc signed one, we would expect macOS to treat the bundle as a different app and require it to establish trust again.

This explains why the app must first be launched successfully. If its main executable is replaced before the first launch, initial validation fails and macOS reports that the app is damaged and should be moved to the Trash. If the executable is replaced after the first launch, however, the modified bundle continues to launch under the original app’s identity.

A Proof-of-Concept Attack#

With these building blocks, we can create a simple proof of concept that archives the application bundle, restores it, replaces its main executable, and launches the modified app.

The replacement executable is ad hoc signed, so it does not inherit the original app’s code-signing identity or previously granted permissions. Attempts to access Keychain items or files protected by TCC still trigger macOS authorization prompts.

For this demonstration, the replacement executable attempts to:

  1. Access Signal’s Keychain item that stores its encryption key.
  2. Access the TCC-protected ~/Desktop and ~/Documents folders.
  3. After collecting the targeted secrets, terminate itself and relaunch the original Signal executable to make the compromise less noticeable.

Although macOS correctly asks the user to approve access, the prompts are misleading. Because the replacement executable resides inside Signal.app, they display Signal’s name and icon and appear to come from the real app.

A user who trusts Signal may approve these requests, believing they came from the original app. Once approved, the replacement executable gains access to the requested resources despite being ad hoc signed and unrelated to Signal’s developer.

When the modified Signal executable requests access to the Desktop folder, macOS displays this prompt:

For comparison, a request from Terminal produces the same macOS authorization prompt:

This proof of concept does not bypass TCC, Keychain protections, or code signing. It instead exploits the user’s trust in an installed app: since the prompts are genuine macOS system prompts, they’re visually indistinguishable from ones generated by the real app.

Here is the proof of concept in action. To make each step easier to follow, the replacement executable displays a window with buttons that trigger individual actions. A real attack could perform these actions automatically in the background, without displaying an interface.

Apple’s Assessment#

Apple concluded that the reported behaviour does not constitute a security issue for the following reasons:

  • The proof of concept replaces the entire application bundle rather than modifying an existing signed executable.
  • The attack requires code execution as the current user and only affects applications owned by that user.
  • The replacement executable does not inherit the original application’s entitlements or previously granted TCC permissions, requiring the user to approve new authorization prompts.
  • Apple considers convincing a user to approve these prompts to be a matter of social engineering rather than a bypass of TCC or other security mechanisms.
  • Gatekeeper is designed to evaluate downloaded applications before their first launch and is not intended to protect files already owned and modified by the current user.

Based on these findings, Apple determined that neither Gatekeeper nor TCC was bypassed and that the report did not require a security fix.

Discussion#

This behaviour does not bypass Gatekeeper or TCC. It does, however, let an attacker silently replace the main executable of a trusted app and exploit that trust through authentic but misleading system prompts. Revalidating modified application bundles or identifying the requesting executable’s code-signing identity in authorization prompts would make the attack far less effective.

  • Revalidate an application’s code signature before launch when its bundle has changed. The brief validation process macOS displays after the modification suggests it has already detected the change.
  • Improve authorization prompts by displaying the code-signing identity (developer or Team ID) of the executable requesting access, not just the application’s name and icon.

Demos & Videos#

Video: Replacing Signal’s Main Executable#

Report Timeline#

Date Event
June 4, 2026 Mysk submits report to Apple.
June 9, 2026 Apple requests more information.
June 11, 2026 Mysk sends the proof-of-concept source code.
July 13, 2026 Mysk asks for an update and discusses possible disclosures to Proton, Signal, Brave, and Mullvad.
July 14, 2026 Apple responds: “We’ve taken an initial pass at reproducing this report.”
July 14, 2026 Apple closes the report.

As a result, trusted applications can be made to execute attacker-controlled code without triggering security warnings when relaunched. Apple assessed the reported behaviour as not requiring a security fix. Affected Platforms# macOS Tahoe 26.0.0 – 26.5.2 macOS Golden Gate 27 beta 1, 2, 3, and 4 Earlier versions of macOS are likely affected as well, but have not been tested. Summary# macOS provides several safeguards to prevent applications and scripts from tampering with other installed applications. In this post, we show a bug in macOS that allows an attacker to: Silently replace an application’s main executable under /Contents/MacOS/ without triggering an authorization prompt. Relaunch the modified application normally, without displaying any security warnings. Impersonate the trusted application in system permission prompts to request access to Keychain secrets and files protected by Transparency, Consent, and Control (TCC), including those inside ~/Desktop and ~/Documents. The attack requires only code execution as the current user, and does not require elevated privileges. Summary for Non-Technical Readers# We found a macOS security issue that Apple looked into but decided not to fix. If you run a malicious app or script on your Mac, an attacker could: Secretly replace trusted apps you already have installed from the web with malicious versions. Request permissions to access private data on behalf of those trusted apps, including files in places you expect to be private, such as your Desktop or Documents folders, or your Keychain. Replacing a trusted app requires neither your Mac’s password nor any special approval, and it can happen entirely in the background. Accessing protected data still requires your approval, but the macOS system prompts show the trusted app’s name and icon, making the requests appear to come from the real app. Background# macOS Application Bundles# On macOS, apps are distributed as application bundles, which are directory structures that appear as a single .app file in Finder. Apple’s documentation describes the bundle format in more detail. After the app is installed (for example, by dragging it into /Applications) and opened, macOS also protects the contents of its bundle. Other applications and scripts, even those running with administrator privileges, cannot modify files inside it. macOS blocks any such attempt and alerts the user that it prevented the modification. macOS alerts the user when an application or script attempts to modify the contents of another application's bundle. The issue is reproducible when: The app was downloaded from the web rather than installed through the Mac App Store. App Store apps are owned by root, while apps downloaded from the web are typically owned by the current user. The app has already been launched at least once, allowing Gatekeeper to complete its initial verification. The attacker already has code execution as the current user, for example through a malicious app or downloaded script. We’ll use Signal as an example. To be clear, this is not a Signal vulnerability. Signal is simply a convenient demonstration target because it is widely trusted and distributed outside the Mac App Store. What’s surprising is that its contents can be modified without triggering an authorization prompt: % touch /Applications/Signal.app/Contents/MacOS/Signal % It works! Video: Archiving and restoring an application bundle allows it to be modified without requiring user authorization. At this point, replacing the app’s main executable is straightforward. I'm Signal!" window.makeKeyAndOrderFront(nil) app.activate(ignoringOtherApps: true) app.run() EOF # Compile it as a command-line macOS executable swiftc -framework Cocoa /tmp/dummy.swift -o /tmp/dummy # Replace the main Signal executable with the dummy binary cp /tmp/dummy /Applications/Signal.app/Contents/MacOS/Signal # Launch the now-modified app (should display a window, not real Signal) open /Applications/Signal.app This behaviour is consistent with how macOS code signing protects application bundles. If the executable is replaced after the first launch, however, the modified bundle continues to launch under the original app’s identity. Replacing the main executable before the application's first launch causes the initial validation to fail. The prompt uses Signal's name and icon, making it appear as though the request originates from the real app. A user who trusts Signal may approve these requests, believing they came from the original app. Once approved, the replacement executable gains access to the requested resources despite being ad hoc signed and unrelated to Signal’s developer. When the modified Signal executable requests access to the Desktop folder, macOS displays this prompt: macOS authorization prompt displayed when the modified Signal executable requests access to the Desktop folder. The prompt is visually indistinguishable from one generated by the legitimate application. For comparison, a request from Terminal produces the same macOS authorization prompt: macOS authorization prompt displayed when Terminal requests access to the Desktop folder. This proof of concept does not bypass TCC, Keychain protections, or code signing. It instead exploits the user’s trust in an installed app: since the prompts are genuine macOS system prompts, they’re visually indistinguishable from ones generated by the real app. Here is the proof of concept in action. To make each step easier to follow, the replacement executable displays a window with buttons that trigger individual actions. A real attack could perform these actions automatically in the background, without displaying an interface. sentences.zh: 访问受保护的数据仍然需要您的批准，但 macOS 系统提示会显示受信任应用程序的名称和图标，使请求看起来像是来自真实的应用程序。

blocks.html_en: Background#

sentences.en: Background#
sentences.zh: 背景＃

blocks.html_en: macOS Application Bundles#

sentences.en: macOS Application Bundles#
sentences.zh: macOS 应用程序包#

blocks.html_en: On macOS, apps are distributed as application bundles, which are directory structures that appear as a single .app file in Finder. An application bundle contains the app’s executable, resources such as icons and artwork, embedded frameworks and libraries, and metadata. Apple’s documentation describes the bundle format in more detail.

sentences.en: On macOS, apps are distributed as application bundles, which are directory structures that appear as a single .app file in Finder.
sentences.zh: 在 macOS 上，应用程序作为应用程序包分发，这些应用程序包是在 Finder 中显示为单个 .app 文件的目录结构。

sentences.en: An application bundle contains the app’s executable, resources such as icons and artwork, embedded frameworks and libraries, and metadata.
sentences.zh: 应用程序包包含应用程序的可执行文件、图标和插图等资源、嵌入式框架和库以及元数据。

sentences.en: Apple’s documentation describes the bundle format in more detail.
sentences.zh: Apple 的文档更详细地描述了捆绑包格式。

blocks.html_en: When a user downloads an app from the Internet and launches it for the first time, macOS verifies its code signature and notarization and applies Gatekeeper policies to determine whether the app is trusted to run.

sentences.en: When a user downloads an app from the Internet and launches it for the first time, macOS verifies its code signature and notarization and applies Gatekeeper policies to determine whether the app is trusted to run.
sentences.zh: 当用户从互联网下载应用程序并首次启动它时，macOS 会验证其代码签名和公证，并应用 Gatekeeper 策略来确定该应用程序是否可信运行。

blocks.html_en: <figcaption>macOS verifying the integrity and notarization of an application before its first launch.</figcaption>

sentences.en: macOS verifying the integrity and notarization of an application before its first launch.
sentences.zh: macOS 在应用程序首次启动之前验证其完整性和公证性。

blocks.html_en: After the app is installed (for example, by dragging it into /Applications) and opened, macOS also protects the contents of its bundle. Other applications and scripts, even those running with administrator privileges, cannot modify files inside it. macOS blocks any such attempt and alerts the user that it prevented the modification.

sentences.en: After the app is installed (for example, by dragging it into /Applications) and opened, macOS also protects the contents of its bundle.
sentences.zh: 安装应用程序（例如，将其拖入 /Applications）并打开后，macOS 还会保护其捆绑包的内容。

sentences.en: Other applications and scripts, even those running with administrator privileges, cannot modify files inside it.
sentences.zh: 其他应用程序和脚本，即使是以管理员权限运行的应用程序和脚本，也无法修改其中的文件。

sentences.en: macOS blocks any such attempt and alerts the user that it prevented the modification.
sentences.zh: macOS 会阻止任何此类尝试，并提醒用户它阻止了修改。

blocks.html_en: <figcaption>macOS alerts the user when an application or script attempts to modify the contents of another application's bundle.</figcaption>

sentences.en: macOS alerts the user when an application or script attempts to modify the contents of another application's bundle.
sentences.zh: 当应用程序或脚本尝试修改另一个应用程序包的内容时，macOS 会向用户发出警报。

blocks.html_en: Archive and Restore#

sentences.en: Archive and Restore#
sentences.zh: 存档和恢复#

blocks.html_en: We accidentally discovered a scenario in which any application or script running with the current user’s privileges can silently replace the main executable of an application bundle and launch the modified application without triggering a code-signature or security warning.

sentences.en: We accidentally discovered a scenario in which any application or script running with the current user’s privileges can silently replace the main executable of an application bundle and launch the modified application without triggering a code-signature or security warning.
sentences.zh: 我们意外地发现了一种场景，其中以当前用户权限运行的任何应用程序或脚本都可以默默地替换应用程序包的主要可执行文件并启动修改后的应用程序，而不会触发代码签名或安全警告。

blocks.html_en: The issue is reproducible when:

sentences.en: The issue is reproducible when:
sentences.zh: 在以下情况下可以重现该问题：

blocks.html_en: • The app was downloaded from the web rather than installed through the Mac App Store. App Store apps are owned by root, while apps downloaded from the web are typically owned by the current user.

sentences.en: The app was downloaded from the web rather than installed through the Mac App Store.
sentences.zh: 该应用程序是从网络下载的，而不是通过 Mac App Store 安装的。

sentences.en: App Store apps are owned by root, while apps downloaded from the web are typically owned by the current user.
sentences.zh: App Store 应用程序由 root 用户拥有，而从网络下载的应用程序通常由当前用户拥有。

blocks.html_en: • The app has already been launched at least once, allowing Gatekeeper to complete its initial verification.

sentences.en: The app has already been launched at least once, allowing Gatekeeper to complete its initial verification.
sentences.zh: 该应用程序已至少启动一次，允许 Gatekeeper 完成其初步验证。

blocks.html_en: • The attacker already has code execution as the current user, for example through a malicious app or downloaded script.

sentences.en: The attacker already has code execution as the current user, for example through a malicious app or downloaded script.
sentences.zh: 攻击者已经以当前用户的身份执行代码，例如通过恶意应用程序或下载的脚本。

blocks.html_en: We’ll use Signal as an example. To be clear, this is not a Signal vulnerability. Signal is simply a convenient demonstration target because it is widely trusted and distributed outside the Mac App Store. The same behaviour affects other apps downloaded from the web, including Brave Browser, Cursor, Mullvad Browser, Proton Mail, Slack, Visual Studio Code, Xcode, and many others.

sentences.en: We’ll use Signal as an example.
sentences.zh: 我们将使用 Signal 作为示例。

sentences.en: To be clear, this is not a Signal vulnerability.
sentences.zh: 需要明确的是，这不是 Signal 漏洞。

sentences.en: Signal is simply a convenient demonstration target because it is widely trusted and distributed outside the Mac App Store.
sentences.zh: Signal 只是一个方便的演示目标，因为它受到广泛信任并分布在 Mac App Store 之外。

sentences.en: The same behaviour affects other apps downloaded from the web, including Brave Browser, Cursor, Mullvad Browser, Proton Mail, Slack, Visual Studio Code, Xcode, and many others.
sentences.zh: 同样的行为也会影响从网络下载的其他应用程序，包括 Brave Browser、Cursor、Mullvad Browser、Proton Mail、Slack、Visual Studio Code、Xcode 等。

blocks.html_en: After installing Signal and launching it once, macOS prevents subsequent modifications to its application bundle. You can verify this by running:

sentences.en: After installing Signal and launching it once, macOS prevents subsequent modifications to its application bundle.
sentences.zh: 安装 Signal 并启动一次后，macOS 会阻止对其应用程序包进行后续修改。

sentences.en: You can verify this by running:
sentences.zh: 您可以通过运行以下命令来验证这一点：

blocks.html_en: Even with sudo, you still cannot modify the application’s main executable:

sentences.en: Even with sudo, you still cannot modify the application’s main executable:
sentences.zh: 即使使用 sudo，您仍然无法修改应用程序的主可执行文件：

blocks.html_en: However, archiving the application bundle with tar, deleting the original, and extracting the archive back into /Applications changes this behaviour:

sentences.en: However, archiving the application bundle with tar, deleting the original, and extracting the archive back into /Applications changes this behaviour:
sentences.zh: 但是，使用 tar 归档应用程序包、删除原始文件并将存档解压回 /Applications 会更改此行为：

blocks.html_en: The restored app launches normally, despite being a different copy of the original bundle. What’s surprising is that its contents can be modified without triggering an authorization prompt:

sentences.en: The restored app launches normally, despite being a different copy of the original bundle.
sentences.zh: 恢复的应用程序正常启动，尽管它是原始捆绑包的不同副本。

sentences.en: What’s surprising is that its contents can be modified without triggering an authorization prompt:
sentences.zh: 令人惊讶的是，它的内容可以在不触发授权提示的情况下进行修改：

blocks.html_en: It works!

sentences.en: It works!
sentences.zh: 有用！

blocks.html_en: <figcaption>Video: Archiving and restoring an application bundle allows it to be modified without requiring user authorization.</figcaption>

sentences.en: Video: Archiving and restoring an application bundle allows it to be modified without requiring user authorization.
sentences.zh: 视频：归档和恢复应用程序包允许在无需用户授权的情况下对其进行修改。

blocks.html_en: At this point, replacing the app’s main executable is straightforward. The modified app continues to launch, and macOS displays neither a Gatekeeper warning nor any indication that its bundle has been altered. Here’s a minimal example that uses Swift to compile a dummy executable and then replaces Signal’s main executable with it:

sentences.en: At this point, replacing the app’s main executable is straightforward.
sentences.zh: 此时，替换应用程序的主要可执行文件就很简单了。

sentences.en: The modified app continues to launch, and macOS displays neither a Gatekeeper warning nor any indication that its bundle has been altered.
sentences.zh: 修改后的应用程序继续启动，macOS 既不显示 Gatekeeper 警告，也不显示其捆绑包已更改的任何指示。

sentences.en: Here’s a minimal example that uses Swift to compile a dummy executable and then replaces Signal’s main executable with it:
sentences.zh: 下面是一个最小的示例，它使用 Swift 编译一个虚拟可执行文件，然后用它替换 Signal 的主可执行文件：

blocks.html_en: This behaviour is consistent with how macOS code signing protects application bundles. The bundle’s resources are sealed by hashes stored in the _CodeSignature/CodeResources file, while the main executable carries its own embedded code signature. Because the archive-and-restore process leaves the bundle resources unchanged, their hashes continue to validate successfully.

sentences.en: This behaviour is consistent with how macOS code signing protects application bundles.
sentences.zh: 此行为与 macOS 代码签名保护应用程序包的方式一致。

sentences.en: The bundle’s resources are sealed by hashes stored in the _CodeSignature/CodeResources file, while the main executable carries its own embedded code signature.
sentences.zh: 捆绑包的资源由存储在 _CodeSignature/CodeResources 文件中的哈希值密封，而主可执行文件则带有自己的嵌入式代码签名。

sentences.en: Because the archive-and-restore process leaves the bundle resources unchanged, their hashes continue to validate successfully.
sentences.zh: 由于归档和恢复过程使捆绑包资源保持不变，因此它们的哈希值将继续成功验证。

blocks.html_en: The replaced executable, however, is ad hoc signed, and macOS permits ad hoc signed executables to run. As a result, the modified application bundle launches successfully even though its original executable has been replaced.

sentences.en: The replaced executable, however, is ad hoc signed, and macOS permits ad hoc signed executables to run.
sentences.zh: 但是，替换的可执行文件是临时签名的，并且 macOS 允许运行临时签名的可执行文件。

sentences.en: As a result, the modified application bundle launches successfully even though its original executable has been replaced.
sentences.zh: 因此，即使原始可执行文件已被替换，修改后的应用程序包也会成功启动。

blocks.html_en: What appears inconsistent is that macOS continues to recognize the modified bundle as the same trusted app — though not entirely, since the replacement executable still triggers Keychain and TCC prompts, as discussed in the next section. Once the original developer-signed executable has been replaced with an ad hoc signed one, we would expect macOS to treat the bundle as a different app and require it to establish trust again.

sentences.en: What appears inconsistent is that macOS continues to recognize the modified bundle as the same trusted app — though not entirely, since the replacement executable still triggers Keychain and TCC prompts, as discussed in the next section.
sentences.zh: 看起来不一致的是，macOS 继续将修改后的捆绑包识别为同一个受信任的应用程序 - 尽管不完全是，因为替换可执行文件仍然会触发钥匙串和 TCC 提示，如下一节所述。

sentences.en: Once the original developer-signed executable has been replaced with an ad hoc signed one, we would expect macOS to treat the bundle as a different app and require it to establish trust again.
sentences.zh: 一旦原始开发人员签名的可执行文件被临时签名的可执行文件替换，我们希望 macOS 将该捆绑包视为不同的应用程序，并要求它再次建立信任。

blocks.html_en: This explains why the app must first be launched successfully. If its main executable is replaced before the first launch, initial validation fails and macOS reports that the app is damaged and should be moved to the Trash. If the executable is replaced after the first launch, however, the modified bundle continues to launch under the original app’s identity.

sentences.en: This explains why the app must first be launched successfully.
sentences.zh: 这解释了为什么应用程序必须首先成功启动。

sentences.en: If its main executable is replaced before the first launch, initial validation fails and macOS reports that the app is damaged and should be moved to the Trash.
sentences.zh: 如果其主要可执行文件在首次启动之前被替换，则初始验证将失败，并且 macOS 报告该应用程序已损坏，应移至垃圾箱。

sentences.en: If the executable is replaced after the first launch, however, the modified bundle continues to launch under the original app’s identity.
sentences.zh: 但是，如果在首次启动后替换了可执行文件，则修改后的捆绑包将继续以原始应用程序的身份启动。

blocks.html_en: <figcaption>Replacing the main executable before the application's first launch causes the initial validation to fail.</figcaption>

sentences.en: Replacing the main executable before the application's first launch causes the initial validation to fail.
sentences.zh: 在应用程序首次启动之前替换主可执行文件会导致初始验证失败。

blocks.html_en: A Proof-of-Concept Attack#

sentences.en: A Proof-of-Concept Attack#
sentences.zh: 概念验证攻击#

blocks.html_en: With these building blocks, we can create a simple proof of concept that archives the application bundle, restores it, replaces its main executable, and launches the modified app.

sentences.en: With these building blocks, we can create a simple proof of concept that archives the application bundle, restores it, replaces its main executable, and launches the modified app.
sentences.zh: 通过这些构建块，我们可以创建一个简单的概念证明，用于归档应用程序包、恢复它、替换其主要可执行文件并启动修改后的应用程序。

blocks.html_en: The replacement executable is ad hoc signed, so it does not inherit the original app’s code-signing identity or previously granted permissions. Attempts to access Keychain items or files protected by TCC still trigger macOS authorization prompts.

sentences.en: The replacement executable is ad hoc signed, so it does not inherit the original app’s code-signing identity or previously granted permissions.
sentences.zh: 替换可执行文件是临时签名的，因此它不会继承原始应用程序的代码签名身份或先前授予的权限。

sentences.en: Attempts to access Keychain items or files protected by TCC still trigger macOS authorization prompts.
sentences.zh: 尝试访问受 TCC 保护的钥匙串项目或文件仍会触发 macOS 授权提示。

blocks.html_en: For this demonstration, the replacement executable attempts to:

sentences.en: For this demonstration, the replacement executable attempts to:
sentences.zh: 对于此演示，替换可执行文件尝试：

blocks.html_en: • Access Signal’s Keychain item that stores its encryption key.

sentences.en: Access Signal’s Keychain item that stores its encryption key.
sentences.zh: 访问 Signal 存储其加密密钥的钥匙串项目。

blocks.html_en: • Access the TCC-protected ~/Desktop and ~/Documents folders.

sentences.en: Access the TCC-protected ~/Desktop and ~/Documents folders.
sentences.zh: 访问受 TCC 保护的 ~/Desktop 和 ~/Documents 文件夹。

blocks.html_en: • After collecting the targeted secrets, terminate itself and relaunch the original Signal executable to make the compromise less noticeable.

sentences.en: After collecting the targeted secrets, terminate itself and relaunch the original Signal executable to make the compromise less noticeable.
sentences.zh: 收集目标机密后，终止自身并重新启动原始 Signal 可执行文件，以使危害不那么明显。

blocks.html_en: Although macOS correctly asks the user to approve access, the prompts are misleading. Because the replacement executable resides inside Signal.app, they display Signal’s name and icon and appear to come from the real app.

sentences.en: Although macOS correctly asks the user to approve access, the prompts are misleading.
sentences.zh: 尽管 macOS 正确地要求用户批准访问，但这些提示具有误导性。

sentences.en: Because the replacement executable resides inside Signal.app, they display Signal’s name and icon and appear to come from the real app.
sentences.zh: 由于替换可执行文件驻留在 Signal.app 内，因此它们显示 Signal 的名称和图标，并且看起来来自真实的应用程序。

blocks.html_en: <figcaption>macOS authorization prompt displayed when the modified Signal executable requests access to Signal's Keychain item. The prompt uses Signal's name and icon, making it appear as though the request originates from the real app.</figcaption>

sentences.en: macOS authorization prompt displayed when the modified Signal executable requests access to Signal's Keychain item.
sentences.zh: 当修改后的 Signal 可执行文件请求访问 Signal 的钥匙串项时，会显示 macOS 授权提示。

sentences.en: The prompt uses Signal's name and icon, making it appear as though the request originates from the real app.
sentences.zh: 该提示使用 Signal 的名称和图标，使其看起来好像请求来自真实的应用程序。

blocks.html_en: A user who trusts Signal may approve these requests, believing they came from the original app. Once approved, the replacement executable gains access to the requested resources despite being ad hoc signed and unrelated to Signal’s developer.

sentences.en: A user who trusts Signal may approve these requests, believing they came from the original app.
sentences.zh: 信任 Signal 的用户可能会批准这些请求，认为它们来自原始应用程序。

sentences.en: Once approved, the replacement executable gains access to the requested resources despite being ad hoc signed and unrelated to Signal’s developer.
sentences.zh: 一旦获得批准，替换可执行文件就可以访问所请求的资源，尽管是临时签名的且与 Signal 开发人员无关。

blocks.html_en: When the modified Signal executable requests access to the Desktop folder, macOS displays this prompt:

sentences.en: When the modified Signal executable requests access to the Desktop folder, macOS displays this prompt:
sentences.zh: 当修改后的 Signal 可执行文件请求访问桌面文件夹时，macOS 会显示以下提示：

blocks.html_en: <figcaption>macOS authorization prompt displayed when the modified Signal executable requests access to the Desktop folder. The prompt is visually indistinguishable from one generated by the legitimate application.</figcaption>

sentences.en: macOS authorization prompt displayed when the modified Signal executable requests access to the Desktop folder.
sentences.zh: 当修改后的 Signal 可执行文件请求访问桌面文件夹时，会显示 macOS 授权提示。

sentences.en: The prompt is visually indistinguishable from one generated by the legitimate application.
sentences.zh: 该提示在视觉上与合法应用程序生成的提示没有区别。

blocks.html_en: For comparison, a request from Terminal produces the same macOS authorization prompt:

sentences.en: For comparison, a request from Terminal produces the same macOS authorization prompt:
sentences.zh: 为了进行比较，来自终端的请求会产生相同的 macOS 授权提示：

blocks.html_en: <figcaption>macOS authorization prompt displayed when Terminal requests access to the Desktop folder.</figcaption>

sentences.en: macOS authorization prompt displayed when Terminal requests access to the Desktop folder.
sentences.zh: 当终端请求访问桌面文件夹时，会显示 macOS 授权提示。

blocks.html_en: This proof of concept does not bypass TCC, Keychain protections, or code signing. It instead exploits the user’s trust in an installed app: since the prompts are genuine macOS system prompts, they’re visually indistinguishable from ones generated by the real app.

sentences.en: This proof of concept does not bypass TCC, Keychain protections, or code signing.
sentences.zh: 此概念验证不会绕过 TCC、钥匙串保护或代码签名。

sentences.en: It instead exploits the user’s trust in an installed app: since the prompts are genuine macOS system prompts, they’re visually indistinguishable from ones generated by the real app.
sentences.zh: 相反，它利用了用户对已安装应用程序的信任：由于提示是真正的 macOS 系统提示，因此它们在视觉上与真实应用程序生成的提示没有区别。

blocks.html_en: Here is the proof of concept in action. To make each step easier to follow, the replacement executable displays a window with buttons that trigger individual actions. A real attack could perform these actions automatically in the background, without displaying an interface.

sentences.en: Here is the proof of concept in action.
sentences.zh: 这是实际的概念验证。

sentences.en: To make each step easier to follow, the replacement executable displays a window with buttons that trigger individual actions.
sentences.zh: 为了使每个步骤更易于遵循，替换可执行文件会显示一个窗口，其中包含触发各个操作的按钮。

sentences.en: A real attack could perform these actions automatically in the background, without displaying an interface.
sentences.zh: 真正的攻击可以在后台自动执行这些操作，而不显示界面。

blocks.html_en: Apple’s Assessment#

sentences.en: Apple’s Assessment#
sentences.zh: 苹果的评估#

blocks.html_en: Apple concluded that the reported behaviour does not constitute a security issue for the following reasons:

sentences.en: Apple concluded that the reported behaviour does not constitute a security issue for the following reasons:
sentences.zh: 苹果公司得出结论，所报告的行为不构成安全问题，原因如下：

blocks.html_en: • The proof of concept replaces the entire application bundle rather than modifying an existing signed executable.

sentences.en: The proof of concept replaces the entire application bundle rather than modifying an existing signed executable.
sentences.zh: 概念验证替换了整个应用程序包，而不是修改现有的签名可执行文件。

blocks.html_en: • The attack requires code execution as the current user and only affects applications owned by that user.

sentences.en: The attack requires code execution as the current user and only affects applications owned by that user.
sentences.zh: 该攻击需要以当前用户身份执行代码，并且仅影响该用户拥有的应用程序。

blocks.html_en: • The replacement executable does not inherit the original application’s entitlements or previously granted TCC permissions, requiring the user to approve new authorization prompts.

sentences.en: The replacement executable does not inherit the original application’s entitlements or previously granted TCC permissions, requiring the user to approve new authorization prompts.
sentences.zh: 替换可执行文件不会继承原始应用程序的权利或之前授予的 TCC 权限，需要用户批准新的授权提示。

blocks.html_en: • Apple considers convincing a user to approve these prompts to be a matter of social engineering rather than a bypass of TCC or other security mechanisms.

sentences.en: Apple considers convincing a user to approve these prompts to be a matter of social engineering rather than a bypass of TCC or other security mechanisms.
sentences.zh: Apple 认为说服用户批准这些提示是社会工程的问题，而不是绕过 TCC 或其他安全机制的问题。

blocks.html_en: • Gatekeeper is designed to evaluate downloaded applications before their first launch and is not intended to protect files already owned and modified by the current user.

sentences.en: Gatekeeper is designed to evaluate downloaded applications before their first launch and is not intended to protect files already owned and modified by the current user.
sentences.zh: Gatekeeper 旨在在首次启动之前评估下载的应用程序，并非旨在保护当前用户已拥有和修改的文件。

blocks.html_en: Based on these findings, Apple determined that neither Gatekeeper nor TCC was bypassed and that the report did not require a security fix.

sentences.en: Based on these findings, Apple determined that neither Gatekeeper nor TCC was bypassed and that the report did not require a security fix.
sentences.zh: 根据这些发现，Apple 确定 Gatekeeper 和 TCC 都没有被绕过，并且该报告不需要安全修复。

blocks.html_en: Discussion#

sentences.en: Discussion#
sentences.zh: 讨论＃

blocks.html_en: This behaviour does not bypass Gatekeeper or TCC. It does, however, let an attacker silently replace the main executable of a trusted app and exploit that trust through authentic but misleading system prompts. Revalidating modified application bundles or identifying the requesting executable’s code-signing identity in authorization prompts would make the attack far less effective.

sentences.en: This behaviour does not bypass Gatekeeper or TCC.
sentences.zh: 此行为不会绕过 Gatekeeper 或 TCC。

sentences.en: It does, however, let an attacker silently replace the main executable of a trusted app and exploit that trust through authentic but misleading system prompts.
sentences.zh: 然而，它确实让攻击者悄悄地替换了受信任应用程序的主要可执行文件，并通过真实但误导性的系统提示来利用这种信任。

sentences.en: Revalidating modified application bundles or identifying the requesting executable’s code-signing identity in authorization prompts would make the attack far less effective.
sentences.zh: 重新验证修改后的应用程序包或在授权提示中识别请求可执行文件的代码签名身份将使攻击的效率大大降低。

blocks.html_en: • Revalidate an application’s code signature before launch when its bundle has changed. The brief validation process macOS displays after the modification suggests it has already detected the change.

sentences.en: Revalidate an application’s code signature before launch when its bundle has changed.
sentences.zh: 当应用程序的捆绑包发生更改时，在启动之前重新验证应用程序的代码签名。

sentences.en: The brief validation process macOS displays after the modification suggests it has already detected the change.
sentences.zh: 修改后 macOS 显示的简短验证过程表明它已经检测到更改。

blocks.html_en: • Improve authorization prompts by displaying the code-signing identity (developer or Team ID) of the executable requesting access, not just the application’s name and icon.

sentences.en: Improve authorization prompts by displaying the code-signing identity (developer or Team ID) of the executable requesting access, not just the application’s name and icon.
sentences.zh: 通过显示请求访问的可执行文件的代码签名身份（开发人员或团队 ID）（而不仅仅是应用程序的名称和图标）来改进授权提示。

content_html_zh:
macOS 中的漏洞允许攻击者悄悄替换从网络下载的任何应用程序的主要可执行文件，而无需提升权限。 因此，可以使受信任的应用程序执行攻击者控制的代码，而不会在重新启动时触发安全警告。 苹果公司评估所报告的行为不需要安全修复。

受影响的平台#

  • macOS 塔霍 26.0.0 – 26.5.2
  • macOS Golden Gate 27 beta 1、2、3 和 4
  • 早期版本的 macOS 也可能受到影响，但尚未经过测试。

概括＃

macOS 提供了多种保护措施来防止应用程序和脚本篡改其他已安装的应用程序。 在这篇文章中，我们展示了 macOS 中的一个错误，该错误允许攻击者：

  • 静默替换 /Contents/MacOS/ 下应用程序的主可执行文件，而不触发授权提示。
  • 正常重新启动修改后的应用程序，不显示任何安全警告。
  • 在系统权限提示中模拟可信应用程序，请求访问受透明、同意和控制 (TCC) 保护的钥匙串机密和文件，包括 ~/Desktop 和 ~/Documents 内的文件。

该攻击只需要以当前用户身份执行代码，不需要提升权限。

非技术读者摘要# 我们发现了一个 macOS 安全问题，Apple 对此进行了调查，但决定不修复。 如果您在 Mac 上运行恶意应用程序或脚本，攻击者可以： 使用恶意版本秘密替换您已从网络安装的受信任应用程序。 代表这些受信任的应用程序请求访问私有数据的权限，包括您希望私有的位置的文件，例如桌面或文档文件夹或钥匙串。 替换受信任的应用程序不需要 Mac 密码，也不需要任何特殊批准，并且可以完全在后台进行。 访问受保护的数据仍然需要您的批准，但 macOS 系统提示会显示受信任应用程序的名称和图标，使请求看起来像是来自真实的应用程序。

背景＃

macOS 应用程序包#

在 macOS 上，应用程序作为应用程序包分发，这些应用程序包是在 Finder 中显示为单个 .app 文件的目录结构。 应用程序包包含应用程序的可执行文件、图标和插图等资源、嵌入式框架和库以及元数据。 Apple 的文档更详细地描述了捆绑包格式。

当用户从互联网下载应用程序并首次启动它时，macOS 会验证其代码签名和公证，并应用 Gatekeeper 策略来确定该应用程序是否可信运行。

安装应用程序（例如，将其拖入 /Applications）并打开后，macOS 还会保护其捆绑包的内容。 其他应用程序和脚本，即使是以管理员权限运行的应用程序和脚本，也无法修改其中的文件。 macOS 会阻止任何此类尝试，并提醒用户它阻止了修改。

存档和恢复#

我们意外地发现了一种场景，其中以当前用户权限运行的任何应用程序或脚本都可以默默地替换应用程序包的主要可执行文件并启动修改后的应用程序，而不会触发代码签名或安全警告。

在以下情况下可以重现该问题：

  1. 该应用程序是从网络下载的，而不是通过 Mac App Store 安装的。 App Store 应用程序由 root 用户拥有，而从网络下载的应用程序通常由当前用户拥有。
  2. 该应用程序已至少启动一次，允许 Gatekeeper 完成其初步验证。
  3. 攻击者已经以当前用户的身份执行代码，例如通过恶意应用程序或下载的脚本。

我们将使用 Signal 作为示例。 需要明确的是，这不是 Signal 漏洞。 Signal 只是一个方便的演示目标，因为它受到广泛信任并分布在 Mac App Store 之外。 同样的行为也会影响从网络下载的其他应用程序，包括 Brave Browser、Cursor、Mullvad Browser、Proton Mail、Slack、Visual Studio Code、Xcode 等。

安装 Signal 并启动一次后，macOS 会阻止对其应用程序包进行后续修改。 您可以通过运行以下命令来验证这一点：

% touch /Applications/Signal.app/Contents/MacOS/Signal
touch: /Applications/Signal.app/Contents/MacOS/Signal: Operation not permitted

即使使用 sudo，您仍然无法修改应用程序的主可执行文件：

% sudo touch /Applications/Signal.app/Contents/MacOS/Signal
touch: /Applications/Signal.app/Contents/MacOS/Signal: Operation not permitted

但是，使用 tar 归档应用程序包、删除原始文件并将存档解压回 /Applications 会更改此行为：

% cd /Applications/
% tar cf .Signal.tar Signal.app
% rm -rf Signal.app
% tar xf .Signal.tar -C /Applications

恢复的应用程序正常启动，尽管它是原始捆绑包的不同副本。 令人惊讶的是，它的内容可以在不触发授权提示的情况下进行修改：

% touch /Applications/Signal.app/Contents/MacOS/Signal
%

有用！

此时，替换应用程序的主要可执行文件就很简单了。 修改后的应用程序继续启动，macOS 既不显示 Gatekeeper 警告，也不显示其捆绑包已更改的任何指示。 下面是一个最小的示例，它使用 Swift 编译一个虚拟可执行文件，然后用它替换 Signal 的主可执行文件：

# Write a minimal Swift program that shows a window
cat << EOF > /tmp/dummy.swift
import Cocoa

let app = NSApplication.shared
let window = NSWindow(
    contentRect: NSRect(x: 0, y: 0, width: 400, height: 200),
    styleMask: [.titled, .closable, .miniaturizable, .resizable],
    backing: .buffered,
    defer: false
)
window.center()
window.title = "Hi! I'm Signal!"
window.makeKeyAndOrderFront(nil)
app.activate(ignoringOtherApps: true)
app.run()
EOF

# Compile it as a command-line macOS executable
swiftc -framework Cocoa /tmp/dummy.swift -o /tmp/dummy

# Replace the main Signal executable with the dummy binary
cp /tmp/dummy /Applications/Signal.app/Contents/MacOS/Signal

# Launch the now-modified app (should display a window, not real Signal)
open /Applications/Signal.app

此行为与 macOS 代码签名保护应用程序包的方式一致。 捆绑包的资源由存储在 _CodeSignature/CodeResources 文件中的哈希值密封，而主可执行文件则带有自己的嵌入式代码签名。 由于归档和恢复过程使捆绑包资源保持不变，因此它们的哈希值将继续成功验证。

但是，替换的可执行文件是临时签名的，并且 macOS 允许运行临时签名的可执行文件。 因此，即使原始可执行文件已被替换，修改后的应用程序包也会成功启动。

看起来不一致的是，macOS 继续将修改后的捆绑包识别为同一个受信任的应用程序 - 尽管不完全是，因为替换可执行文件仍然会触发钥匙串和 TCC 提示，如下一节所述。 一旦原始开发人员签名的可执行文件被临时签名的可执行文件替换，我们希望 macOS 将该捆绑包视为不同的应用程序，并要求它再次建立信任。

这解释了为什么应用程序必须首先成功启动。 如果其主要可执行文件在首次启动之前被替换，则初始验证将失败，并且 macOS 报告该应用程序已损坏，应移至垃圾箱。 但是，如果在首次启动后替换了可执行文件，则修改后的捆绑包将继续以原始应用程序的身份启动。

概念验证攻击#

通过这些构建块，我们可以创建一个简单的概念证明，用于归档应用程序包、恢复它、替换其主要可执行文件并启动修改后的应用程序。

替换可执行文件是临时签名的，因此它不会继承原始应用程序的代码签名身份或先前授予的权限。 尝试访问受 TCC 保护的钥匙串项目或文件仍会触发 macOS 授权提示。

对于此演示，替换可执行文件尝试：

  1. 访问 Signal 存储其加密密钥的钥匙串项目。
  2. 访问受 TCC 保护的 ~/Desktop 和 ~/Documents 文件夹。
  3. 收集目标机密后，终止自身并重新启动原始 Signal 可执行文件，以使危害不那么明显。

尽管 macOS 正确地要求用户批准访问，但这些提示具有误导性。 由于替换可执行文件驻留在 Signal.app 内，因此它们显示 Signal 的名称和图标，并且看起来来自真实的应用程序。

信任 Signal 的用户可能会批准这些请求，认为它们来自原始应用程序。 一旦获得批准，替换可执行文件就可以访问所请求的资源，尽管是临时签名的且与 Signal 开发人员无关。

当修改后的 Signal 可执行文件请求访问桌面文件夹时，macOS 会显示以下提示：

为了进行比较，来自终端的请求会产生相同的 macOS 授权提示：

此概念验证不会绕过 TCC、钥匙串保护或代码签名。 相反，它利用了用户对已安装应用程序的信任：由于提示是真正的 macOS 系统提示，因此它们在视觉上与真实应用程序生成的提示没有区别。

这是实际的概念验证。 为了使每个步骤更易于遵循，替换可执行文件会显示一个窗口，其中包含触发各个操作的按钮。 真正的攻击可以在后台自动执行这些操作，而不显示界面。

苹果的评估#

苹果公司得出结论，所报告的行为不构成安全问题，原因如下：

  • 概念验证替换了整个应用程序包，而不是修改现有的签名可执行文件。
  • 该攻击需要以当前用户身份执行代码，并且仅影响该用户拥有的应用程序。
  • 替换可执行文件不会继承原始应用程序的权利或之前授予的 TCC 权限，需要用户批准新的授权提示。
  • Apple 认为说服用户批准这些提示是社会工程的问题，而不是绕过 TCC 或其他安全机制的问题。
  • Gatekeeper 旨在在首次启动之前评估下载的应用程序，并非旨在保护当前用户已拥有和修改的文件。

根据这些发现，Apple 确定 Gatekeeper 和 TCC 都没有被绕过，并且该报告不需要安全修复。

讨论＃

此行为不会绕过 Gatekeeper 或 TCC。 然而，它确实让攻击者悄悄地替换了受信任应用程序的主要可执行文件，并通过真实但误导性的系统提示来利用这种信任。 重新验证修改后的应用程序包或在授权提示中识别请求可执行文件的代码签名身份将使攻击的效率大大降低。

  • 当应用程序的捆绑包发生更改时，在启动之前重新验证应用程序的代码签名。 修改后 macOS 显示的简短验证过程表明它已经检测到更改。
  • 通过显示请求访问的可执行文件的代码签名身份（开发人员或团队 ID）（而不仅仅是应用程序的名称和图标）来改进授权提示。
